page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is a compute-optimized server instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 176 virtual CPUs mapped to 88 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 146478.58 and a PassMark CPU score of 105096.7. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching top-tier speeds of 458.9 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 11970.72 GB/sec for cached small-block reads. Single-core performance is average, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1731.0. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to top-tier performance in encryption, physics simulations, and ray tracing.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high compute capacity relative to its memory footprint. This makes it a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory allocations or local storage. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and the low 2.0 GB RAM per vCPU ratio represent clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring high-speed local disk caching.

The instance is suited for highly parallelized tasks such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 126.23 GB/sec, and high-throughput database operations like Redis, which reached 164.37 million SET operations per second. It is also capable of handling medium-scale LLM inference, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 176 vCPUs (88 physical cores) powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor at 2.7 GHz, paired with 352 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core performance remains average, its massive parallel processing capabilities yield top-tier results in database operations, static web serving, and cryptography. The 2.0 GB memory-per-vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff, optimizing the instance for high-density compute workloads rather than memory-heavy applications. It is suited for parallelized workloads, Redis deployments, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 176 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-176 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 176 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM, built for high-throughput parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform delivering top-tier multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 176 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors.
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory read/write bandwidth.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for static web serving, Redis, and software compilation.
